justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Authentication per consumer type correctly designed — JWT/OAuth2 for mobile, API keys for partners, mTLS for internal. (2) Internal traffic separation fully addressed with dedicated Internal Service Mesh Gateway on private subnet, network-level isolation. (3) Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway in multiple places including pitfalls_to_avoid. (4) SPOF risk identified for both Identity Service (critical) and Redis/rate limiting. (5) Webhook delivery for partners addressed with dedicated Webhook Delivery Service including retry, exponential backoff, dead-letter queue. (6) flexibility_points (4 items) and rigidity_points (3 items) present and well-reasoned. (7) Technology recommendation includes build/buy consideration — mentions Kong, Envoy, AWS API Gateway and recommends using proven frameworks rather than building from scratch.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough consumer-segmented gateway design with separate gateways per consumer type — goes beyond the basic requirement
Internal traffic isolation addressed at network level (private subnet) not just software level, which is a stronger architectural choice
Business logic exclusion explicitly called out in component boundaries, pitfalls_to_avoid, and architecture patterns
Webhook delivery fully designed with async dispatch, retry with exponential backoff and jitter, per-partner circuit breaker, dead-letter queue, and rate limiting
Build vs buy trade-off addressed — recommends proven gateway frameworks while listing multiple options (Kong, Envoy, AWS API Gateway)
Phased migration plan with shadow mode reduces big-bang deployment risk
Fail-open rate limiting is a thoughtful availability-over-correctness trade-off with clear rationale
SPOF risks identified for multiple components (Identity Service rated critical, Redis rated high, gateway bottleneck rated medium) with concrete mitigations
Gateway latency overhead per hop (1-5ms) not explicitly quantified as a trade-off, though rate limit latency of ~1-2ms is mentioned

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Authentication per consumer type correctly designed — JWT/OAuth2 for mobile, API key for partners with dedicated listeners. (2) Internal traffic separation fully addressed with network-isolated internal listener on private VPC CIDR. (3) Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway in multiple places (component boundaries, pitfalls_to_avoid). (4) SPOF risk identified as critical with active-active multi-AZ mitigation. (5) Webhook delivery for partners comprehensively addressed with dedicated Webhook Dispatcher, at-least-once semantics, exponential backoff, DLQ, and HMAC signatures. (6) flexibility_points (5) and rigidity_points (3) present and well-reasoned. (7) Technology recommendation discusses multiple options (Kong, Envoy/Ambassador, AWS API Gateway, custom) with explicit acknowledgment that choice depends on deployment platform — build/buy consideration addressed.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough three-listener gateway model with clear component boundaries and responsibilities
Header sanitization as a security invariant is a strong detail — prevents identity spoofing
Phased migration plan using Strangler Fig pattern with observe-only phase shows operational maturity
Rate limiter fail-open with local fallback counters addresses Redis dependency risk well
Webhook delivery design is production-grade: HMAC signatures, exponential backoff, DLQ, partner dashboard, poll fallback
Trade-offs section is exceptionally detailed with concrete alternatives considered for each decision
JWT revocation delay risk identified with practical mitigation (short-lived tokens + blocklist via pub/sub)
Gateway latency per hop (1-5ms typical) not explicitly quantified in trade-offs, though implicitly addressed via architecture choices
Domain model with bounded contexts and context map goes beyond what was required, adding useful architectural clarity

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Authentication per consumer type (JWT for mobile, API key for partners) correctly designed with canonical identity envelope. Internal traffic separation fully addressed with dedicated Internal Gateway on private network. Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway — authorization and domain logic kept in downstream services. SPOF risk identified as critical with three-layer mitigation. Webhook delivery for partners comprehensively addressed with async dispatcher, retry logic, and per-partner circuit breakers. flexibility_points and rigidity_points present and well-reasoned. Technology recommendation includes multiple options (Kong, Envoy, AWS API Gateway, custom) with clear build/buy consideration.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough internal vs external traffic separation with physical network isolation, not just logical
Explicitly calls out that authorization must NOT be in the gateway — only authentication
Shadow-mode migration strategy addresses the real-world risk of auth cutover
Webhook design uses transactional outbox pattern with per-partner circuit breakers and queue isolation
Rate limiting designed per principal identity with configurable tiers, not hardcoded
Fail-open rate limiting trade-off explicitly analyzed with fallback strategy
Phased implementation guidance with concrete timelines and file paths
Pitfalls section covers API key hashing, per-partner rate limiting, and avoiding dual-auth during migration
